SiteNotes: Mini plot, only 10 by 10 was able to be surveyed due to emergency evacuation (sickness). Lower quality data, but data nonetheless. Site was extremely homogenous in an open herbaceous meadows below the upper part of the ridge between Lone Goat and Red Mountain. Original lat/long was incorrect, it would have placed us on top of the ridge, with a slope too flat, instead of the upper slope underneath the ridge. Plot is located directly below orginal lat/long. Elevation, slope, aspect, mesoslope, surface shape, and community composition match well. Relocation confidence: High/ Med-High.

Relocation confidence: Med. Lat/long/elevation are clearly wrong for the site description given. There is no area in this mountain range with an area of 685m and the lat/long given is not at the basin of Lone Goat and Red Mountain, like described in the notes. This suggests a possible data entry error from the original survey. We are confident we are in the righ general area of the original plot as there is only 1 basin, and very few areas that can match the slope/ aspect/ community composition listed from the original survey. This site was mesic and extremely shrubby (mostly salix), with some drier patches. Appears to be a snowmelt patch/ runoff area. Due to sickness, we had to evacuate early and only managed to survey 1/2 of the plot. It is lower quality data, but data nonetheless.
VegNotes: Platanthera sp. Same at the other plot surveyed on this trip. MITEPEN is the mitella sp. found at the other plots on this trip. 2 types of SALIX SP.present at this site: one with hairy leaves, and one with hairless leaves, however, we lumped them together. Vacciniums were hard to resolve, potential hybrids at this site.

We are confident we are in the righ general area of the original plot as there is only 1 basin, and very few areas that can match the slope/ aspect/ community composition listed from the original survey. This site was mesic and extremely shrubby (mostly salix), with some drier patches. Appears to be a snowmelt patch/ runoff area. Due to sickness, we had to evacuate early and only managed to survey 1/2 of the plot. It is lower quality data, but data nonetheless.
VegNotes: Platanthera sp. Same at the other plot surveyed on this trip. MITEPEN is the mitella sp. found at the other plots on this trip. 2 types of SALIX SP.present at this site: one with hairy leaves, and one with hairless leaves, however, we lumped them together. Vacciniums were hard to resolve, potential hybrids at this site.
